raw_response:
I agree with the consolidation but would like to add an additional point regarding why option C might be more precise than just focusing on 'specific micro-organisms.' While recognizing that not all plaque is necessarily harmful supports option A, specifying that only certain microbes cause caries provides a clearer link directly related to the development of cavities rather than other forms of gum disease. Therefore, although option B also correctly identifies some aspects of the hypothesis, option C offers a slightly narrower yet more focused definition aligned with the primary concern of cariogenesis.

Answer Choice: C Based on this analysis, my final selection stands firm with **Option C**. The emphasis on specific microbes causing caries aligns better with the core aspect highlighted in the Specific Plaque Hypothesis concerning tooth decay mechanisms.
